prompt: Design and implement a Go REST API for a task manager. It should support CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete) for tasks. Each task has: id (string), title (string), description (string), status (pending/in_progress/done), created_at (time). Use net/http only (no frameworks). Include a go.mod file. The API should listen on port 8080.

Design and implement a Go CLI tool that reads a JSON file of key-value pairs and outputs them as environment variable exports. Input: config.json with arbitrary nested keys. Output: KEY=value format, with nested keys joined by underscore. Include a go.mod file. Handle errors gracefully.

Implement a concurrent worker pool in Go that processes jobs from a channel. Requirements: configurable number of workers, graceful shutdown via context cancellation, error collection, and a results channel. Include unit tests that verify: pool starts N workers, all jobs are processed, shutdown stops workers cleanly. Include go.mod.

Implement a Go package that provides an in-memory LRU cache with: Get(key) (value, bool), Set(key, value), Delete(key), Len() int. The cache must be thread-safe and have a configurable max capacity. When capacity is exceeded, evict the least recently used entry. Include comprehensive unit tests. Include go.mod.

Design and implement a Go event-driven pipeline processor. Architecture: Source (reads events from a channel) -> Processor (transforms events, can be chained) -> Sink (writes results). Requirements: generic types, backpressure via buffered channels, metrics (events processed, errors, duration), graceful shutdown. Include unit tests for the pipeline with at least 3 processors chained. Include go.mod.
